alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a methoxy group attached to the benzene ring of a phenol moiety.
alternative_parents.name: Methoxyphenols

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a methoxybenzene or a derivative thereof.
alternative_parents.name: Anisoles

alternative_parents.description: Aromatic compounds contaning a phenoxy group.
alternative_parents.name: Phenoxy compounds

alternative_parents.description: Bromophenols carrying a iodine at the C4 position of the benzene ring.
alternative_parents.name: P-bromophenols

alternative_parents.description: Organic aromatic compounds containing a monocyclic benzene moiety carrying exactly or one more methoxy groups.
alternative_parents.name: Methoxybenzenes

alternative_parents.description: Phenols that a unsubstituted at the 2-position.
alternative_parents.name: 1-hydroxy-2-unsubstituted benzenoids

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing the alkyl aryl ether functional group with the generic formula R-O-R' , where R is an alkyl group and R' is an aryl group.
alternative_parents.name: Alkyl aryl ethers

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a bromine atom attached to a benzene ring.
alternative_parents.name: Bromobenzenes

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing the acyl bromide functional group.
alternative_parents.name: Aryl bromides

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing an amide derivative of carboxylic acid, with the general structure RN(R1)C(R2)=O (R1-R2 any atom but H).
alternative_parents.name: Tertiary carboxylic acid amides

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a bond between carbon a pnictogen atom. Pnictogens are p-block element atoms that are in the group 15 of the periodic table.
alternative_parents.name: Organopnictogen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a carbonyl group, with the general structure RC(=O)R', where R=organyl, R'=H, N, O, organyl group or halide group.
alternative_parents.name: Carbonyl compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a nitrogen atom.
alternative_parents.name: Organonitrogen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an oxide group.
alternative_parents.name: Organic oxides

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a chemical bond between a carbon atom and a bromine atom.
alternative_parents.name: Organobromides

class.description: Isoquinoline alkaloids with a skeleton deriving from lycorine, norbelladine, homolycorine, hemanthamine, crinine, tazettine, montanine, plicamine, galanthindole, galanthamine, gracilline, among others. Amaryllidaceae Alkaloids represent a large group of biogenetically related isoquinoline alkaloids that are found exclusively in plants belonging to this family of Amaryllidaceae. This group of alkaloids is also derived from two tyrosine units which combine, with loss of one carbon atom, to give a benzylphenylethylamine precursor unit, e.g. Norbelladine, which by various oxidative cyclisation processes, prominent among which are phenol oxidative coupling reactions, can give rise to the nine major skeletal groups.
class.name: Amaryllidaceae alkaloids

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as norbelladine-type amaryllidaceae alkaloids. These are amaryllidaceae alkaloids compounds containing the norbelladine skeleton.  They are derived initially from the condensation of tyramine and protocatechuic aldehyde or its derivatives in plants.

direct_parent.description: Amaryllidaceae alkaloids compounds containing the norbelladine skeleton.  They are derived initially from the condensation of tyramine and protocatechuic aldehyde or its derivatives in plants.
direct_parent.name: Norbelladine-type amaryllidaceae alkaloids

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

subclass.description: Amaryllidaceae alkaloids compounds containing the norbelladine skeleton.  They are derived initially from the condensation of tyramine and protocatechuic aldehyde or its derivatives in plants.
subclass.name: Norbelladine-type amaryllidaceae alkaloids

superclass.description: Naturally occurring chemical compounds that contain mostly basic nitrogen atoms. This group also includes some related compounds with neutral and even weakly acidic properties. Also some synthetic compounds of similar structure are attributed to alkaloids. In addition to carbon, hydrogen and nitrogen, alkaloids may also contain oxygen, sulfur and more rarely other elements such as chlorine, bromine, and phosphorus.
superclass.name: Alkaloids and derivatives
